title: Setting Up and Use the SiteFlow Tally Integration
metaTitle: Setting Up and Use the SiteFlow Tally Integration
metaDescription: Connect SiteFlow to Tally and push Sales Invoices, Material Purchases, Payments, and Subcon Bills directly into Tally as vouchers. Covers agent setup, ledger mapping, and daily sync.
author: SiteFlow Operations Team
body:
Setting up the SiteFlow-Tally integration and push transactions from SiteFlow directly into Tally as vouchers. This covers the one-time setup (connection, agent installation, ledger mapping, party mapping, cost centre mapping, and bank mapping), and the daily workflow for pushing records to Tally.

What the Integration Does

SiteFlow’s Tally integration pushes approved transactions from SiteFlow into Tally as vouchers automatically. This eliminates double data entry between SiteFlow and your accounting software.

Transactions pushed from SiteFlow appear in Tally’s Day Book with the party name, amount, project reference, and narration details.

Prerequisites

  • You need Admin access in SiteFlow
  • Tally must be installed and open on the machine that will act as the agent
  • Have your Tally company name ready — it must be entered exactly as it appears in Tally (case sensitive)
  • All SiteFlow bank accounts should be configured in Settings before mapping banks to Tally ledgers
  • Decide the Sync Window start date — this is the earliest transaction date that will be available to push. Set it to your financial year start to include all records from that date.

Transaction Types Supported

Accessing the Tally Integration

Open your Tally integration page in a browser:

sync.siteflow.com/tally/your-username

Replace “your-username” with your SiteFlow account username.

The page shows tabs for each transaction type: Sales Invoices, Material Sales, Material Purchases, Subcon Expenses, Equipment Expenses, Other Expenses, Site Expenses, Payments In, Payments Out, and P2P Payments.

Top toolbar buttons:

Part 1: One-Time Setup: Connection

Go to Tally Settings (top right of the integration page).

Click the Connection tab.

Click Save.

⚠️ The authentication token occasionally expires. If records stop syncing, return to Connection and re-verify your mobile number.

Part 2: One-Time Setup: Tally Agent

The Tally Agent is a small program that runs on the computer where Tally is installed. It receives push requests from SiteFlow and posts them into Tally. The agent must be running for any push to work.

Installing the Agent

  1. Click Download Agent (Windows .zip) and install it on the machine running Tally
  2. In the Register a New Agent section, enter a machine label (e.g. “Office-PC” or “Accountant Laptop”)
  3. Click Generate Key
  4. Copy the key and paste it into the agent installer on the Tally machine
  5. The agent appears in Registered Agents with status Active

💡 Only one active agent per machine is needed. The agent must be running in the background whenever you push records to Tally. If the machine is off or the agent is closed, records will queue but not post.

⚠️ If the agent shows Revoked, generate a new key in Tally Settings under Agents and reconfigure the agent on the machine.

Part 3: One-Time Setup: Voucher Type and Ledger Mapping

Click the Accounts tab in Tally Settings.

For each SiteFlow transaction type, map it to the correct Tally Voucher Type and Ledger.

Mode controls how items are posted in Tally:

Additional settings on the Accounts tab:

Click Save Accounts when done.

Part 4: One-Time Setup: Party Mapping

Click the Parties tab in Tally Settings.

All SiteFlow parties (vendors, clients, subcontractors) are listed here. Each must be mapped to the corresponding ledger in Tally before records involving that party can be pushed.

Step 1: Auto Match

Click Auto match by exact name.

SiteFlow will attempt to match parties with the same name in Tally automatically.

Step 2: Manual Match Remaining Parties

For any parties that were not matched automatically (names differ between SiteFlow and Tally), select the correct Tally ledger from the dropdown next to each party.

Step3: Save

Click Save Parties.

⚠️ Unmapped parties block the push. If a party is not mapped when you push, SiteFlow shows a warning. You can choose to auto-create the ledger in Tally or map the party first and retry.

💡 Run Refresh Integration Checks after adding any new party in SiteFlow or Tally to detect new unmapped parties before they cause a push failure.

Part 5: One-Time Setup: Cost Centres

Click the Cost Centres tab in Tally Settings.

Cost centres allow project-wise expense tracking in Tally. Each SiteFlow project is mapped to a Tally Cost Centre.

  1. Enable the Tag vouchers with Cost Centres (per-project) toggle
  2. For each SiteFlow project, select the corresponding Tally Cost Centre from the dropdown
  3. Click Save Cost Centres

⚠️ If cost centre mapping is not configured, vouchers are posted to Tally without any project tag. Even if your Tally company has cost centres enabled, they will not be applied unless this mapping is completed.

Part 6: One-Time Setup: Banks and Cash

Click the Banks and Cash tab in Tally Settings.

Each SiteFlow bank account is listed. Map each to the corresponding ledger in Tally.

  1. Find the bank account (e.g. HDFC Bank — A/c: 1234567890)
  2. Select the matching Tally bank ledger from the dropdown
  3. Click Save Banks

💡 If a payment record has no bank account linked, it posts to the Default cash ledger configured in the Accounts section. Ensure the default cash ledger is set correctly before pushing any payment records.

Part 7: Pushing Records to Tally (Daily Workflow)

Once setup is complete, follow this process each time you want to push transactions:

Step 1: Fetch Latest Records

Click Fetch from SiteFlow to load the latest approved transactions.

Step 2: Review Records

Each tab shows the following columns for each transaction:

Step 3: Select and Push

Tick the checkboxes next to the records you want to push. Click Select All to push everything in the current tab.

Click Push Selected to Tally.

A results dialog appears for each record:

Step 4: Verify in Tally

Open Tally and go to Day Book. Each pushed record appears with the party name, amount, and narration containing the SiteFlow project and reference details.

Tips

  • Set the Sync Window date to your financial year start during initial setup. This makes all records from that date available to push. It cannot be moved backward later without re-verification.
  • Run Refresh Integration Checks any time a new party, project, or bank account is added in either SiteFlow or Tally. This detects new unmapped items before they block a push.
  • Use Auto-create missing ledgers carefully. It is helpful for keeping pushes moving but creates ledgers automatically in Tally. If SiteFlow party names differ from Tally ledger names, auto-create may add duplicates.
  • The agent must be running on the Tally machine at the time of push. Records stay in Queued state until the agent comes online. If records stay Queued for more than a few minutes, check that Tally is open and the agent is active.
  • Push records regularly rather than accumulating weeks of backlog. Large batches take longer and make it harder to investigate individual push failures.
  • Material Purchase uses Itemwise mode. This posts each material line item separately in Tally, which is important for maintaining accurate stock ledger entries. All other expense types use Lumpsum mode.

If Something Goes Wrong
